title: erlangea schebellensis
intro: Erlangea schebellensis is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of wild sage. Traditionally, Erlangea schebellensis has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, coughs, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and aiding in digestion. Erlangea schebellensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts for consumption, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
